findings.evidence: Lemmas use `variable {K L : Type*} ...` and namespace TauCeti.IntermediateField; existing dir TauCeti/FieldTheory/IntermediateField/Quadratic.lean uses the same namespace; the analogous generic helper TauCeti.Polynomial.CardRootSetUnion lives in Algebra/Polynomial, not EffectiveBounds.
findings.fix: Move both generic lemmas to a new file under TauCeti/FieldTheory/IntermediateField/ (mirroring Quadratic.lean and the namespace); keep only the ℚ/bounded-polynomial specialization (finite_boundedRoots and the two ..._root_natDegree_le_abs_intCoeff... theorems) here, importing the moved file.
findings.issue: finite_setOf_exists_mem_eq_adjoin_simple_of_finite and ncard_setOf_exists_mem_eq_adjoin_simple_le are fully generic over arbitrary K L, sit in namespace TauCeti.IntermediateField, depend only on Set.Card and IntermediateField.Adjoin.Basic, and are documented as the reusable counting step — yet live in a roadmap-specific EffectiveBounds file.

summary: The two generic intermediate-field counting lemmas (arbitrary K L, namespace TauCeti.IntermediateField, no EffectiveBounds dependency) are broadly reusable but buried in an EffectiveBounds file; per the repo's own convention (cf. generic CardRootSetUnion living in Algebra/Polynomial) they belong under TauCeti/FieldTheory/IntermediateField. Imports are all direct and used.
